3D & Animation

PixyCAD is a native 3D CAD app for Mac and iPad, built for makers, designers and 3D-printing enthusiasts who want professional solid modelling without traditional CAD complexity. It combines an approachable modelling workflow with a professional geometry foundation powered by Parasolid. We are launching on Product Hunt to get feedback from people who care about CAD, 3D printing and better creative tools on Apple platforms.

Hi Product Hunt — I’m Andrea, from PixyCAD team We built PixyCAD because many 3D CAD tools still feel too complex for makers and 3D-printing enthusiasts, especially when working on modern Apple devices. Mac and iPad users deserve a native modelling experience that feels approachable without giving up a professional solid-modelling foundation. PixyCAD is a native CAD app for Mac and iPad, powered by Parasolid. It supports workflows from sketching to solid modelling, with Apple Pencil support on iPad and export formats including STEP, Parasolid, STL and 3MF. PixyCAD is free to try with the Starter plan, and today we are mainly looking for honest feedback. Thank you for taking a look. [...] Launch Team

results.title: 10 Best 3D CAD Software for 3D Printing [Free and Paid] - Blog - Meshy
results.content:
Platform availability. SolidWorks is Windows-only. If you need online CAD software for 3D printing, web-based tools like TinkerCAD, Meshy, and SketchUp Free run anywhere with a browser. Shapr3D is the only professional CAD that works natively on iPad. Blender, FreeCAD, and OpenSCAD cover all three desktop OSes.
 Community and learning resources. When you get stuck, community size determines how fast you recover. Blender and Fusion 360 have massive tutorial ecosystems. OpenSCAD has dedicated maker libraries (BOSL2, NopSCADlib). Smaller tools with less community support can feel isolating. [...] What sets it apart: The only professional CAD tool designed from the ground up for iPad and Apple Pencil. Recent updates added Wrap & Emboss for projecting sketches onto curved surfaces. Models are print-ready by default because Parasolid produces watertight solids.

Key Features:

 Touch-based and Apple Pencil modeling with Parasolid precision
 Wrap & Emboss for surface detailing on curved faces
 Cross-platform: iPadOS, macOS, and Windows with the same feature set
 Exports to STL, 3MF, STEP, OBJ, USDZ, GLB, DWG, DXF
 Both parametric and direct modeling workflows

Pros:

 Exceptionally intuitive. Design anywhere on iPad or desktop.
 Professional modeling kernel ensures print-ready geometry by default
 All modeling tools available on the free plan

Cons: [...] Rating: ~4.5/5 (G2, over 1300 reviews)

### Shapr3D

Shapr3D is a professional CAD application built around touch-based modeling on iPad with Apple Pencil, powered by the Siemens Parasolid kernel (the same engine used in SolidWorks and NX). Because Parasolid enforces solid geometry by construction, your models are inherently watertight, no third-party repair tools needed before printing. For hobbyists designing custom cases, brackets, or replacement parts, you can go from sketch to STL export on the couch without opening a laptop.

results.title: 3D Modeling Software for Mac - MakerWorld General - Bambu Lab Community Forum
results.content:
Mac actually has some solid 3D options — depending on how deep you want to go:

Beginner / Fast Design

Shapr3D — total Mac/iPad native, super intuitive  
(Apple Pencil + iPad combo is chef’s kiss)

Tinkercad — browser-based, basic but great for quick shapes

Serious CAD

Fusion 360 — great all-rounder, runs on Mac  
(Not fully native but works really well, especially for mechanical parts)

Onshape — browser CAD, surprisingly powerful, team-friendly

FreeCAD — free/open-source, a little clunky but capable once you get it

Organic / Artistic

(Not fully native but works really well, especially for mechanical parts)

Not sure what your LLM means by this – Fusion is fully Mac native and supports Apple Silicon. Great free choice for personal use. [...] I think others have covered the conventional recommendations so I’ll through some unconventional ones your way.

Both are more geared towards ease of use and artistic modeling. So if you’re looking for parametric based stick to the CAD recommendations.

Freecad is very good now and very much like Solidworks but free. Tinkercad has its uses but if you want any degree of accuracy, Freecad parametric is the way to go.

I use the free version of Fusion360 on my Mac. Shaper3d on the ipad.

@Sebo3D1

Why do you have a closed source printer? (I assume you do, as you’re here)

Because it gives me the best results while still allowing me to maintain full privacy.

results.title: Shapr3D: 3D Modeling software | Windows & Mac & iPad
results.content:
Explore case studies to learn how teams of all sizes are successfully using Shapr3D.

#### What devices does Shapr3D run on?

Shapr3D runs natively on Windows PCs and Windows tablets, Apple macOS computers, iPads, and Apple Vision Pro. It’s one of the only natively built iPad CAD applications out there, earning it an Editor’s Choice award on the App Store for iPad.

Check out our supported devices and system requirements.

#### Does it require specialized hardware to run Shapr3D?

No. Shapr3D runs on all modern devices, and it doesn’t require a specialized CAD computer. See our list of compatible devices.

#### Is Shapr3D a cloud-based CAD system? [...] Shapr3D is CAD that’s simple enough for beginners and powerful enough for engineers.  
Download it on Mac, iPad, or Windows for quick, intuitive 3D modeling.

Download for free

Download for free

, or XT and STEP to connect with other CAD systems, simulation tools, or manufacturing processes.

### A geometrically sound environment.

Your workflow rests on a solid foundation: Shapr3D is built with the Siemens Parasolid® kernel under the hood.

### Design with your favourite tools.

Shapr3D supports SpaceMouse, Wacom tablets, custom keybindings, and Apple Pencil, delivering a flexible modeling experience tailored to your preferences.

Fast concepts

### Design anywhere. [...] #### Is Shapr3D a cloud-based CAD system?

No. Shapr3D is a downloadable CAD that runs natively on all devices, saving designs locally and backing up to the Shapr3D Cloud for you to access designs instantly from anywhere. You can work across any of your devices or go offline, without needing internet.

results.title: Best CAD App for iPad in 2026
results.content:
## Verdict: Which iPad CAD App Should You Use?

If you want an iPad CAD app that feels built for makers, works well with Apple Pencil, exports proper 3D printing files, and doesn’t force you into a desktop-heavy or cloud-heavy workflow, PixyCAD is the standout choice.

It hits a sweet spot that is still surprisingly rare: serious solid modeling, approachable pricing, and a workflow that feels natural on both iPad and Mac.

Shapr3D is still a strong option, especially for professionals. Part3D is a good entry point for beginners. Fusion 360 remains excellent on desktop, but less compelling if your focus is truly iPad-first design.

If you already have an iPad and Apple Pencil, the easiest way to judge is simple: try PixyCAD for yourself and see how quickly it clicks. [...] PixyCAD is built for the kind of workflow a lot of makers actually want: sketch something quickly, turn it into a solid model, tweak it with precision, and export a clean file for printing.

Under the hood, it uses Parasolid-based BREP solid modeling, which is a big deal if geometry quality matters to you. STEP exports are clean, STL output is reliable, and the whole experience feels closer to professional CAD than to simplified hobby software.

Just as important, the iPad experience feels intentional. Apple Pencil isn’t treated like a secondary input method. Sketching, selecting, and editing all feel natural, which makes a huge difference on a touchscreen device. [...] Run natively on iPad
 Export STL or 3MF for slicers, and ideally STEP for interoperability
 Work well with Apple Pencil, not just basic touch controls
 Feel approachable enough for hobbyists, while still being capable enough for real projects
 Avoid unnecessary friction like forced cloud dependence or enterprise-style pricing

Here are the iPad CAD apps that stood out.

## The Best iPad CAD Apps for 3D Printing, Compared

### PixyCAD - Best Overall iPad CAD App for Makers

Price: Free (Starter, 2 projects) / $17.99/month or $179.99/year (Maker)  
Platform: iPad + Mac  
Exports: STL, 3MF, STEP, Parasolid X\_T  
Works offline: Yes, fully on-device
